description_long: This house features a stone exterior with a mix of gray brickwork and red-tiled gable roofs, giving it a historic, European appearance. The structure is composed of two main towers of differing heights, one with a steeply pitched roof and a weather vane, and the other with a flat top. Large arched windows with wooden frames are symmetrically arranged on the front facade, while a set of double wooden doors with glass panels serves as the main entryway. The roof sections vary in pitch, with one side showing mossy textures, suggesting age and weathering. Architectural details include decorative window trims, a small chimney, and a prominent overhanging roof section. The overall design evokes a blend of Gothic Revival and Victorian influences, suitable for a residential neighborhood or a historic urban district.
description: A stone house with two towers, red-tiled gable roofs, arched windows, and a detailed wooden entryway, blending Gothic Revival and Victorian styles.
description_view_0: Side view showing the narrow profile of the house, with the taller tower and a red-tiled roof section protruding outward.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the main entryway, symmetrical arched windows, and both towers with distinct rooflines.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the flat side of the taller tower and the red-tiled roof section.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the back of the house, with fewer windows, a chimney, and the continuation of the red-tiled roof.
attribute_entryway_design: Double wooden doors with glass panels, arched transom, and a small overhanging roof section
